titleStmt.title: Letter from F. A. Downing, County Solicitor, to Sir Matthew Nathan, 16 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: F. A. Downing

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from F. A. Downing, county solicitor for Kerry, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), under-secretary for Ireland. The writer is representing Kerry County Council in seeking adjustment of the rules for appointment of management committee members to the Tralee and Dingle Light Railway. He requests a copy of the relevant Rules and Regulations.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/19613/2

correspAction.placeName: 22 Denny Street, Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle. 16th November 1915 Tralee & Dingle Light Railway Dear Sir, The above Railway was constructed under the Tramways (Ireland) Act
and is now managed by a Committee of the County Council approved under various Orders
of the Privy Council. I have been instructed by the County Council to apply
for a new Order amending the last Order which regulates the appointment of
the committee of Management, and I shall feel obligated if you will send me a
copy of the Rules and Regulations under the Tramways Act dealing with the
obtaining of Privy Council Orders under that Act, so that I may proceed to
obtain the new Order required by the County Council. Yours Faithfully,
F. A. Downing
